text:
Diesel cracks at historical highs across all regions.

China's not going help this time.

#oott https://t.co/ZVQrgdQB3j

COLUMN: The end of the US-Iran war has brought oil down to ~$75 a barrel. But the escalation of the Russia-Ukraine war is inflating the cost of petroleum refined products. The result? Fuel prices remain as high as if crude was still above $100. 

@Opinion https://t.co/DMRh8JH6xF

GLOBAL REFINING margins are nearing record highs following the breakdown of the ceasefire between the United States and Iran, and Ukraine’s attacks on Russia’s oil refineries. In the United States, the crack spread for turning three barrels of WTI crude into two barrels of gasoline and one barrel of diesel has averaged $61 per barrel so far in July. After adjusting for inflation, the benchmark 3-2-1 crack is the second-highest on record after $64 in June 2022, shortly after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine and the imposition of sanctions by the United States and Europe in response:
This oil market is so far from normal, words cannot describe what I’m seeing.
In a normal oil market, if Kharg Island even got a whiff of being attacked, +$15/bbl.

Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: war on.

No visible inbound tankers in the last 24 hours.

Reports stated that Iran fired at two vessels today. There was an escort that saw tankers exit, ATARAKTOS, was one of the VLCCs that exited.

With no diplomatic resolution for the Oman lane, we will likely see more escalation throughout this week. 

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker list.

Production shut-in excluding Iran is back to -7.5 million b/d. 

If the inbound tanker count remains low, we will be back to -9 million b/d by the end of the month.
"passage through the #Strait_of_Hormuz is currently unfeasible." #oott
Oil demand forecasts in mbpd over time (IEA) #oott https://t.co/ubJLXNojRL

Water levels at a key waypoint on the Rhine River have fallen to the lowest for the time of year in decades as Europe struggles with intense heat waves, limiting fuel flows into southern Germany and Switzerland.

Levels this month at the closely watched Kaub chokepoint that’s west of Frankfurt have slid below those in 2022, when drought disrupted commercial navigation on rivers across Europe. The barge clearance level there is at its lowest for this time of year since at least 1990, according to the earliest German federal data compiled by Bloomberg.
#oott https://t.co/kTBI8kcRkG

UKMTO has received a report of an incident 50NM south of Aden, Yemen.

A tanker travelling east in the IRTC has reported being approached by 6 small boats approaching from the starboard side. One small boat has closed to 5 cables and the AST on board the tanker has fired warning shots. The other 5 small boats have remained at 1NM.

Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson says mediators are still continuing their efforts to mediate between Iran and US, including in recent days.

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei also says Tehran is trying to agree on a joint mechanism with Oman for the Strait of Hormuz, but claims US pressure on Oman has hindered efforts.
https://t.co/j9VHr6IPaa

Explosions of unknown origin were heard in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, a news agency reports, following an exchange of attacks between Tehran and Washington.

“Media and residents reported having heard on Monday at midday explosions near Bandar Abbas and the island of Qeshm,” Mehr news agency said, adding that the blasts “appear to be coming from the West Coast of Bandar Abbas.”
https://t.co/lAQPlfiLCB
Italy's Eni, Seri launch battery venture to market energy storage systems https://t.co/Npv6piuNMN

Iran's IRGC announces that ground forces have destroyed two US HIMARS missile platforms and ammunition depots at a US ground-to-ground missile base in Kuwait, in the fourth phase of its response to today's US attacks, per Fars. @HormuzLetter

Iran's IRGC announces the full destruction of fuel tanks and Patriot air defense systems at the US Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait, and an FPS strategic radar system at the Ahmed Al-Jaber Air Base in Kuwait, in the third phase of its response to today's US strikes, per Fars. @HormuzLetter

A source close to Iran's Ghalibaf says in the past two hours Iran's IRGC "has fired at several US vessels attempting to approach Iranian shores," along with "two rogue" commercial vessels. @HormuzLetter

The source says "the Strait of Hormuz is now completely closed off from all routes and will not reopen in the medium term," adding that "attacks will expand and the conflict will deepen." @HormuzLetter
7 commercial crossings of the Strait of Hormuz in the last 24 hours - Bloomberg @M_McDonough https://t.co/bVG5w21OZq
Iran's largest petrochemical hub in Mahshahr, Khuzestan Province, Iran's main oil region, has been hit by US airstrikes. @HormuzLetter
COT update: Dollar long hits decade high as funds buy agriculture and cut energy and metals exposure https://t.co/X5MkquGZ89 via @saxobank
